title: Failed config validation with custom CNI plugins
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Adding a custom CNI plugin fails configuration validation

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Add a file `/etc/cni/net.d/10-demo-custom-cni.conflist` with the contents (contents are valid for the `custom-cni` plugin:

```
{
  "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
  "name": "demo",
  "plugins": [{
    "type": "custom-cni",
    "arg": "value"
  }]
}
```

3. Place `custom-cni` binary in `/usr/local/lib/cni/` (which is listed in `/etc/containers/libpod.conf` under `cni_plugin_dir`) (also safe to assume `custom-cni` is functioning properly)


**Describe the results you received:**

Running `podman network ls` lists the `demo` network, but also shows a warning message:

```
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/10-demo-custom-cni.conflist: [netplugin failed: "Error: missing field `name` at line 1 column 22\n"]
NETWORK ID    NAME        VERSION     PLUGINS
2f259bab93aa  podman      0.4.0       bridge,portmap,firewall,tuning
3ecee7bc9885  demo          0.4.0       custom-cni
```

Trying to run a container with that network also fails, as it appears that warning turns into a hard error.

```
 podman run --net=demo --rm -d docker.io/nginxdemos/hello:latest
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/10-demo-custom-cni.conflist: [netplugin failed: "Error: missing field `name` at line 1 column 22\n"]
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/10-demo-custom-cni.conflist: [netplugin failed: "Error: missing field `name` at line 1 column 22\n"]
ERRO[0000] error loading cached network config: network "demo" not found in CNI cache
WARN[0000] falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/10-demo-custom-cni.conflist: [netplugin failed: "Error: missing field `name` at line 1 column 22\n"]
ERRO[0000] Error tearing down partially created network namespace for container 76f09c099080ff4870cb95c8019e1751fc38688f4e39d70607c38b88317f1bb3: CNI network "demo" not found
Error: error configuring network namespace for container 76f09c099080ff4870cb95c8019e1751fc38688f4e39d70607c38b88317f1bb3: CNI network "demo" not found
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The network is created, and control is handed to `custom-cni` to set up the namespace and interfaces for the container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

    codename: jammy

  idMappings:
    gidmap: null

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/jammy,now 3.4.4+ds1-1ubuntu1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Troubleshooting yes, Podman 4.x no as our system at work uses official repositories which don't have that version yet.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

The example above is running inside a LXD VM, but that seems unrelated to the problem at hand.

comments.body: Please try 4.0 - we did a full rewrite of our network stack in 4.x, which may have resolved the problem.

Podman 4.0 will still validate cni configs.

The validation is done by the cni lib so I think either your plugin or config are not correct?

Yeah same result on 4.x:

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri May  6 16:15:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

The plugin was written by us, so I know the config is correct for the plugin. What I can't figure out is where this `missing "name" field` is coming from, or where it's expecting that to be? It looks like it's wanting it to be in the CNI network config, but you can clearly see the "name" field is there.

I don't mind digging through the source of the cni lib, do you have a link to where I could find it?
I think the error is coming from your plugin. Does it implement all the required commands?

The reason I think it's not that and that it's coming from somewhere in Podman (or related libs) is running the plugin manually works (i.e. if do `podman run --net=none ...` and then `cat /etc/cni/net.d/10-demo-custom-cni.conflist | CNI_COMMAND=ADD CNI_ARGS=... CNI_NETNS=... CNI_IFNAME=... /usr/local/lib/custom-cni`).
Well you only test the ADD command? Does you plugin implement the VERSION command?

Also I do not think that using the full conflist for the plugin is correct, the cni lib will send a different config to the plugin not the original, check https://github.com/containernetworking/cni/blob/main/SPEC.md

I am closing this since the issue is not related to podman but fell free to continue the conversation.

> Well you only test the ADD command? Does you plugin implement the VERSION command?

Yes the other commands (`DEL`, `CHECK`, `VERSION`) are implemented too (well `CHECK` is stubbed, but that should not be getting called for something as simple as `podman network ls` which still generates the warning).

> [cni lib sending a different config to the plugin]

Yeah, but even there [it says the `name` field is pulled from the CNI Network config](https://github.com/containernetworking/cni/blob/main/SPEC.md#deriving-execution-configuration-from-plugin-configuration).

Edit: To be clear too, I'm  *not* ruling out something the plugin is or isn't passing as being the issue. Just my initial thoughts were that it is/was unlikely. I appreciate the help too 😄

Just to close the loop it turned out it was the plugin expecting a field that shouldn't have been there. My test didn't catch it cause I was manually writing what I *thought* Podman was sending, not what it was actually sending. A quick `strace` to see what Podman was sending cleared it up.

I appreciate all those that took time out of their day to assist and make suggetions!
